iran_nuke.notes: Day 15 04:04 — FALLING STABLE. No new nuclear signals. Conventional war intensifying but nuclear track unchanged. Iran reportedly rejected Putin's proposal to move enriched uranium to Russia (JPost). Parchin bunker penetration confirmed. Nuclear track continues de-escalation. 15 stable.

iran_conventional.notes: Day 15 04:04 — IMMINENT RISING. MAJOR ESCALATION: (1) NEW: Five US Air Force KC-135 refueling planes damaged by Iranian missile strike on the ground at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia — WSJ/Reuters. First direct Iranian strike on US military assets on Saudi soil. No fatalities reported but planes undergoing repairs. (2) Iran fired 500+ ballistic/naval missiles + ~2000 drones since Feb 28 (Fars News). (3) Qatar intercepts — reports emerge Qatar may have struck back, shooting down 2 Iranian Su-24 bombers (JPost/denied by Qatar MFA). (4) Canada commits 23.6M barrels to IEA record 400M barrel strategic reserve release (Hindu). (5) 1,444 killed, 18,551 injured in US-Israel strikes per Iran Health Ministry (Al Jazeera). (6) Hegseth says US 'decimating' Iranian military (ABC7). 100 capped.

israel_lebanon.notes: Day 15 04:04 — IMMINENT RISING. MAJOR ESCALATION: Axios reports Israel planning 'massive ground invasion of Lebanon' — largest since 2006. Senior Israeli official: 'We are going to do what we did in Gaza.' (2) Israeli strikes in south Lebanon killed 14 medical workers, injured 6 more in al-Sawaneh and Borj Qalaouiye (NYT/Lebanon Health Min). (3) 2 Israeli soldiers killed, 14 wounded in southern Lebanon (CNN) — first significant IDF casualties. (4) Hezbollah Radwan forces attempted raids on northern Israel. (5) 773+ dead in Lebanon. 96→100 (capped).

turkey.notes: CRITICAL — RISING STABLE. Third intercept confirmed. No 4th intercept this cycle — key threshold watch continues. Article 4 pressure steady. No change. 53 stable.

india.notes: Stable deterrent. No new signals. IndiGo fuel surcharge from Iran crisis — economic impact only. No escalation indicators.

russia.notes: Day 15 04:04 — STABLE. US sanctions license operational. Trump reportedly rejected Putin's proposal to move Iranian enriched uranium to Russia (JPost). Putin-Trump call covered Iran + Ukraine. No new Russia-specific signals this cycle. 34→32 slight dip.

china.notes: Day 15 04:04 — STABLE. Warplane flights near Taiwan plunge from 92 to 7. $14B Taiwan arms deal awaits Trump approval. China 'rebuilding grip on North Korea.' No new escalation signals.

north_korea.notes: Stable elevated. KCNA condemns Japan Type-12 missile deployment. Kim Jong Un + daughter gun practice photos. China-DPRK engagement increasing. 16 stable.

russia_ukraine.notes: Day 15 04:04 — RISING STABLE. US sanctions license operational. Zelensky: US sought postponement of trilateral talks. Trump deal stalling further as Iran war dominates. No new Ukraine-specific developments this cycle. 35 stable.

pakistan_afghanistan.notes: No new reports in this cycle. Saudi-Pakistan Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement active. China mediating. Stable elevated.

changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: CRITICAL ESCALATION: Day 15 04:04 — TWO MAJOR DEVELOPMENTS: (1) IRAN STRIKES US ASSETS ON SAUDI SOIL: Five KC-135 refueling planes damaged at Prince Sultan Air Base. Direct Iranian strike on US military hardware on allied territory — qualitatively different from Gulf state missile attacks. (2) ISRAEL PLANNING MASSIVE LEBANON GROUND INVASION: Axios reports largest ground operation since 2006, with explicit 'Gaza model' language from senior Israeli officials. South Lebanon strikes kill 14 medical workers. IDF suffers first significant casualties (2 KIA, 14 wounded). NUCLEAR TRACK: Still de-escalating at 15%. Iran reportedly rejected Putin's uranium transfer proposal. KEY WATCH FOR NEXT 6H: (1) US response to KC-135 strike at Saudi base — potential trigger for regime change rhetoric. (2) Lebanon ground invasion timeline — imminent deployment? (3) 4th Turkey intercept → Article 4 trigger. (4) Gulf states response to sustained attacks. (5) Hormuz status. (6) IEA reserve release impact on oil prices.

latest_news.headline: Five US Air Force KC-135 refueling planes damaged by Iranian missile strike at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia
latest_news.text: Five US Air Force KC-135 refueling planes were struck and damaged on the ground at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia during an Iranian missile strike, WSJ reported citing two US officials. No fatalities reported but planes undergoing repairs. First direct Iranian strike on US military assets on Saudi soil. This is a qualitative escalation — hitting US hardware on allied territory.

latest_news.headline: Israel planning 'massive ground invasion of Lebanon' — largest since 2006
latest_news.text: Israel is planning a massive ground invasion of Lebanon, Axios reports, which would be the largest Israeli ground operation since 2006. Senior Israeli official told Axios: 'We are going to do what we did in Gaza,' referring to the flattening of buildings Israel says Hezbollah uses. The invasion would drag Lebanon to the epicenter of the escalating war with Iran.

latest_news.headline: Israeli strikes in south Lebanon kill 14 medical workers, injure 6
latest_news.text: Lebanon's health ministry said Israeli strikes in the south of the country killed 14 medical workers and injured six more. The strikes took place in the towns of al-Sawaneh and Borj Qalaouiye. Significant civilian casualty event as ground invasion planning confirmed.

latest_news.headline: Two Israeli soldiers killed, 14 wounded in southern Lebanon
latest_news.text: Two Israeli soldiers have been killed and at least 14 wounded in southern Lebanon, CNN reports. Hezbollah's elite Radwan forces have attempted raids on northern Israel. First significant Israeli military casualties in the Lebanon theater.

latest_news.headline: Iran has fired 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2000 drones since Feb 28
latest_news.text: Military source told Fars News Agency that Iran has fired over 500 ballistic and naval missiles and almost 2,000 drones since February 28. Almost 40% of launches aimed toward Israel, almost 60% fired toward US targets. Massive sustained barrage over 2 weeks of conflict.

latest_news.headline: IEA agrees record 400M barrel strategic reserve release — Canada commits 23.6M barrels
latest_news.text: The IEA has agreed to release a record 400 million barrels of oil from strategic stockpiles held by member nations to combat price spikes from the Iran war. Canada will support with 23.6 million barrels from domestic producers, Energy Minister Tim Hodgson said.

latest_news.headline: Trump reportedly rejects Putin's proposal to move Iranian enriched uranium to Russia
latest_news.text: Western diplomats and sources told JPost that Trump rejected Putin's proposal to move Iran's enriched uranium to Russia as a de-escalation measure. This closes a potential diplomatic off-ramp for the nuclear dimension of the conflict.

latest_news.headline: Hegseth says US 'decimating' Iranian military, 'don't need to worry' about Strait of Hormuz
latest_news.text: US Defense Secretary Hegseth said the US is 'decimating' Iranian military capability and that Americans 'don't need to worry' about the Strait of Hormuz. Statements suggest no near-term diplomatic off-ramp on Hormuz.

outlook: CRITICAL — Global war probability RISING to 65%. Day 15 04:04 — TWO MAJOR ESCALATIONS: (1) IRAN STRIKES US ASSETS IN SAUDI ARABIA: Five US KC-135 refueling planes damaged at Prince Sultan Air Base by Iranian missile strike. This is a qualitative escalation — direct Iranian strike on US military hardware on allied territory, not just Gulf state civilian targets. (2) ISRAEL PLANNING MASSIVE LEBANON GROUND INVASION: Axios confirms Israel planning largest ground operation since 2006, with explicit 'Gaza model' language. South Lebanon strikes kill 14 medical workers. IDF suffers first significant casualties (2 KIA, 14 wounded). NUCLEAR TRACK: Still de-escalating at 15%. Iran reportedly rejected Putin's uranium transfer proposal, closing a diplomatic off-ramp. Iran has now fired 500+ missiles and ~2000 drones since Feb 28. IEA agrees record 400M barrel reserve release to stabilize oil. KEY WATCH FOR NEXT 6H: (1) US military response to KC-135 strike at Saudi base — potential regime change trigger. (2) Lebanon ground invasion timeline — imminent deployment? (3) 4th Turkey intercept → Article 4 trigger. (4) Gulf states joint defense activation after UAE + Qatar + Saudi strikes. (5) Hormuz status. (6) Oil price impact of IEA reserve release. (7) Iran nuclear posture as conventional war intensifies.
